title: Food Satiety Index Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Satiety index for 24+ foods (Holt 1995). White bread = 100. Values >150 = high satiety. Choose high SI foods to stay full longer between meals.
intro: The **Satiety Index (SI)** was developed by Susanna Holt at the University of Sydney in 1995. It measures how full a food makes you feel 2 hours after eating, compared to white bread (=100). Boiled potatoes rank highest at 323, while croissants score only 47. This calculator lets you check the satiety index of 24 common foods—perfect for choosing meals that keep you satisfied longer and support weight loss goals.
keyTakeaway: **Boiled potatoes (SI=323)** are the most filling food; **croissants (SI=47)** are the least. Use high SI foods to avoid hunger between meals and reduce overall calorie intake by 20–30%.
useCases: You're trying to lose weight and want to choose foods that keep you satisfied longer.
You arrive at lunch hungry and want to improve your breakfast to break the cycle.
You're a student or office worker looking for snacks that actually last until your next meal.
You pack school lunches and want your kids to stay full, not snacking all day.
You're an athlete in a cutting phase and need maximum appetite control.

dataUpdate.notes: Data from Holt et al. 1995, Satiety Index of Common Foods.

example.title: Example: Boiled Potato vs. Croissant
steps: The potato fills you up **~7× more** than a croissant at the same calories.
This explains why diets rich in starches tend to reduce hunger.
example.result: A boiled potato is **7× more filling** than a croissant.

explanation:
## What is the Satiety Index?

The **Satiety Index (SI)** measures how full you feel after eating 240 calories of a food, compared to white bread (=100). Developed by Holt et al. in 1995 at the University of Sydney.

## Complete Holt 1995 Food Chart

| Food | SI |
|---|---|
| Boiled Potato | **323** |
| White Fish | 225 |
| Oatmeal | 209 |
| Orange | 202 |
| Apple | 197 |
| Whole Wheat Pasta | 188 |
| Lean Beef | 176 |
| Whole Wheat Bread | 154 |
| Egg | 150 |
| Cheese | 146 |
| White Rice | 138 |
| Lentils | 133 |
| Banana | 118 |
| Sugary Cereal | 118 |
| French Fries | 116 |
| White Bread (ref.) | **100** |
| Ice Cream | 96 |
| Yogurt | 88 |
| Peanuts | 84 |
| Chocolate | 70 |
| Donut | 68 |
| Cake | 65 |
| Croissant | **47** |

## Why Potatoes Are So Filling

1. High volume density (water + fiber).
2. Low caloric density (~87 kcal per 100g).
3. **Resistant starch** when cooled.

## Factors That Increase Satiety

- **Protein**: 20+ grams per meal.
- **Fiber**: 5+ grams per serving.
- **Volume and water content**.
- **Prolonged chewing**.

## Factors That REDUCE Satiety

- Added sugar.
- Fat + sugar combinations (chocolate, ice cream).
- Liquid calories (soda, juice).
- Ultra-processed foods.

## Practical Use

For weight loss, aim for 60% of daily calories from foods with SI >150. Consult a registered dietitian.

faq.q: What is the satiety index and why does it matter?
faq.a: The satiety index (SI) measures how full a food makes you feel relative to white bread. Higher SI foods help you eat fewer total calories, making weight loss easier and more sustainable.

faq.q: Why are boiled potatoes the most filling food?
faq.a: Potatoes rank highest (SI=323) due to their high water and fiber content, low calorie density, and resistant starch when cooled—all factors that fill your stomach without excess calories.

faq.q: Is white bread low in satiety?
faq.a: White bread is the reference point (SI=100). It's not inherently low-satiety, but many foods are significantly more filling, especially whole grains, proteins, and vegetables.

faq.q: Why do croissants score so low on the satiety index?
faq.a: Croissants have SI=47 because they combine refined flour, added fat, and sugar—highly processed ingredients that don't trigger fullness signals in your brain.

faq.q: Can I use the satiety index for weight loss?
faq.a: Yes. Eating high SI foods automatically reduces your overall calorie intake by 20–30% because you feel fuller longer and snack less between meals.

faq.q: What's the difference between satiety and hunger?
faq.a: Satiety is the feeling of fullness after eating. Hunger is the desire to eat. High SI foods increase satiety, reducing hunger—helping you eat less without feeling deprived.

faq.q: How can I improve the satiety of my meals?
faq.a: Add protein (20+ grams), increase fiber intake (vegetables, legumes), drink water with meals, and chew slowly. Avoid sugary, ultra-processed foods.

faq.q: Does satiety index work for people trying to maintain weight?
faq.a: Absolutely. High SI foods naturally regulate appetite, preventing overeating and making weight maintenance easier than counting calories alone.

sources.name: Holt et al. 1995 - Satiety Index of Common Foods
